page: Microsoft Azure F72s_v2 is a compute-optimized virtual machine belonging to the FSv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 72 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 36 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor operating at 2.6 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 35 MB L3 cache. Memory allocation is 144.0 GB, which translates to a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. Local storage consists of a 590 GB volume, and the instance is Premium Storage capable. No GPU accelerators are present.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 67087.24 and a PassMark CPU score of 50595.14, both placing in the top 25% of tested systems. Memory bandwidth is also strong, reaching 3735.49 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 206.9 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. However, single-core performance is average, as shown by a Geekbench single-core score of 1252.0 and a stress-ng single-core score of 1454.7. Large language model text generation represents a notable performance tradeoff, with text generation speeds for a 7B model dropping to a weak 6.5 tokens/sec, while prompt processing for the same model remains average at 100.59 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the F72s_v2 offers high compute density relative to its memory footprint, making it a viable option for CPU-bound workloads that do not require massive RAM arrays. The instance is suited for multi-threaded tasks such as software compilation, ray tracing, and multi-threaded decompression, where it achieves average to strong benchmark results. It is also applicable for static web serving and database operations, though memory-intensive databases may be constrained by the 2.0 GB per core memory ratio.
description: Microsoft Azure F72s_v2 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the FSv2 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 72 dedicated vCPUs and 36 physical cores. Operating at 2.6 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, it features 144.0 GB of RAM (2.0 GB per core) and 590 GB of local storage. Benchmarks show strong multi-core capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 67087.24 and high memory bandwidth, though single-core performance and LLM text generation speeds are average to weak. This instance provides high compute density relative to memory, making it a cost-efficient choice for parallelized CPU-bound workloads. It is suited for software compilation, ray tracing, multi-threaded decompression, and static web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F72s_v2 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 72 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 144 GB of RAM, and 590 GB of local storage, delivering strong multi-core performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F72s_v2 is a compute-optimized VM with 72 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 144 GB RAM, and 590 GB local storage for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A high-density compute instance featuring seventy-two dedicated processor cores and balanced local storage for parallelized, CPU-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 72 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 144 GB of system memory
590 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong multi-core CPU performance and high memory bandwidth
Suited for software compilation, ray tracing, and multi-threaded decompression
Average single-core and weak LLM text generation performance
